data.title: Minecraft Damage Calculation
data.solution:
m=int(input())
a,e=map(int,input().split())
b,f=map(int,input().split())
c,g=map(int,input().split())
d,h=map(int,input().split())
n=round((m*(100-(a+b+c+d)*4)/100)*(100-(e+f+g+h)*4)/100,2)
print("{:.2f}".format(n))
print("Alive"if n<20 else"Dead")
data.statement:
Damage in the game <<Minecraft>> is calculated in various ways. However, this puzzle will focus on just <<armor points>> and the <<protection enchantment level>>, meaning that the other protection types, armor toughness, and any others should be ignored.

You will be given {{5}} lines of input, with <<Line 1>> representing the [[initial damage]] dealt and the <<remaining 4 lines>> each corresponding to the [[armor points]] and [[protection level]] {{(prot for short)}} of each armor piece {{(Helmet, Chestplate, Leggings, and Boots)}}. You need to output the [[remaining damage]] that is dealt to the player after all the damage reduction steps have been executed. Then also output {{Alive}} if the [[remaining damage]] is < {{20.00}} or {{Dead}} if not. <<(that means a [[remaining damage]] of 20.00 is considered {{Dead}})>>

<<NOTE:>> Be sure to perform all calculations using full precision, then round the final result ([[remaining damage]]) to {{2}} decimal places. Use this rounded value of [[remaining damage]] in the "{{Alive}} or {{Dead}}" condition check.

<<Damage Reduction Steps (in order):>>

<<Step 1: Armor reduction>>
Each armor point provides a {{4%}} reduction. Armor points are addictive across all armor pieces.
{{[[post-armor damage]] = [[initial damage]] x (100% - 4% x total [[armor points]])}}

<<Step 2: Protection reduction>>
Each protection level provides a {{4%}} reduction. Protection levels are addictive across all armor pieces.
{{[[remaining damage]] = [[post-armor damage]] x (100% - 4% x total [[protection level]])}}

<<Example:>>
```
             Armor points    Protection level
Helmet            2                 2
Chestplate        6                 4
Leggings          5                 4
Boots             1                 3
                -----             -----
Total            14                13
```
[[post-armor damage]]
{{= [[initial damage]] x (100% - 4% x 14)}}
{{= [[initial damage]] x 44%}}

[[remaining damage]]
{{= [[post-armor damage]] x (100% - 4% x 13)}}
{{= [[post-armor damage]] x 48%}}

And like always, good luck! ;)

<<Line 1:>> [[remaining damage]] dealt to the player after armor point and protection level calculations rounded to {{2}} decimal places <<(add trailing zeros if necessary)>>
<<Line 2:>> Output {{Alive}} if [[remaining damage]] < {{20.00}}. Otherwise, output {{Dead}}.

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

Damage in the game Minecraft is calculated in various ways. However, this puzzle will focus on just armor points and the protection enchantment level, meaning that the other protection types, armor toughness, and any others should be ignored.

You will be given 5 lines of input, with Line 1 representing the initial damage dealt and the remaining 4 lines each corresponding to the armor points and protection level (prot for short) of each armor piece (Helmet, Chestplate, Leggings, and Boots). You need to output the remaining damage that is dealt to the player after all the damage reduction steps have been executed. Then also output Alive if the remaining damage is < 20.00 or Dead if not. (that means a remaining damage of 20.00 is considered Dead)

NOTE: Be sure to perform all calculations using full precision, then round the final result (remaining damage) to 2 decimal places. Use this rounded value of remaining damage in the "Alive or Dead" condition check.

Damage Reduction Steps (in order):

Step 1: Armor reduction
Each armor point provides a 4% reduction. Armor points are addictive across all armor pieces.
post-armor damage = initial damage x (100% - 4% x total armor points)

Step 2: Protection reduction
Each protection level provides a 4% reduction. Protection levels are addictive across all armor pieces.
remaining damage = post-armor damage x (100% - 4% x total protection level)

Example:

             Armor points    Protection level
Helmet            2                 2
Chestplate        6                 4
Leggings          5                 4
Boots             1                 3
                -----             -----
Total            14                13

post-armor damage
= initial damage x (100% - 4% x 14)
= initial damage x 44%

remaining damage
= post-armor damage x (100% - 4% x 13)
= post-armor damage x 48%

And like always, good luck! ;)




   

      
Input

      
Line 1: An integer representing initial damage dealt (aka full damage)
Line 2: Two space-separated integers representing armor points and protection level for the helmet
Line 3: Two space-separated integers representing armor points and protection level for the chestplate
Line 4: Two space-separated integers representing armor points and protection level for the leggings
Line 5: Two space-separated integers representing armor points and protection level for the boots

   

   

      
Output

      
Line 1: remaining damage dealt to the player after armor point and protection level calculations rounded to 2 decimal places (add trailing zeros if necessary)
Line 2: Output Alive if remaining damage < 20.00. Otherwise, output Dead.

   

   

      
Constraints

      
1 ≤ initial damage ≤ 300
0 ≤ armor points for helmet and boots ≤ 3
0 ≤ armor points for chestplate ≤ 8
0 ≤ armor points for leggings ≤ 6
0 ≤ protection level for all pieces ≤ 4
protection level will always be 0 if the corresponding armor piece is missing (i.e. armor points for piece = 0)
Output: remaining damage > 0.00

   

   

      
Example

      

         

            
Input

            
4
1 4
3 4
2 4
1 4

         

         

            
Output

            
1.04
Alive
